  • Method
Module com.cuatrifasico.battleshi
Package com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.view.board

Class BoardTheme

java.lang.Object
com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.view.board.BoardTheme
public final class BoardTheme extends Object
Centralizes every geometric measurement used to build board and ship shapes programmatically. Only geometry lives here: colors are intentionally NOT defined as Java constants anymore. They live in styles.css as regular JavaFX CSS classes, since fill colors do not participate in any shape-construction math and are better kept re-skinnable without recompiling.

Sizes/arcs stay in Java because they ARE consumed by arithmetic when procedurally building shapes (cell positions, ship end-cap unions). In particular, once an end cap is built via Shape.union(javafx.scene.shape.Shape, javafx.scene.shape.Shape), the resulting node is a generic Shape backed by a path — it no longer exposes an arcWidth/arcHeight property, so CSS could never have driven that rounding in the first place.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final double
    CELL_ARC
    Corner radius used for board cells, frigates, and aircraft carrier end caps.
    static final double
    CELL_GAP
    Total visual gap perceived between two adjacent water cells (split as half-padding on each cell).
    static final double
    CELL_SIZE
    Full logical size of one cell.
    static final String

    static final String

    MISS_ARM_LENGTH
     
    static final double
    MISS_STROKE_WIDTH
     
    static final double
    SHIP_CAPSULE_ARC
    Corner radius used for destroyer/submarine end caps, producing a full semicircle ("capsule") look.
    static final double
    TRAY_SHIP_SPACING
    Spacing (horizontal and vertical) between ship entries in the fleet tray.
    static final double
    TURN_INDICATOR_MARGIN
    Margin between the 10x10 grid block and the turn-indicator border around it.

  • Field Details

    • CELL_SIZE

      public static final double CELL_SIZE
      Full logical size of one cell. Ships and the coordinate grid both use this directly (no gap).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values
